chunks.heading: V0.5.0 Overview
chunks.text: This is the documentation for version 0.5.0 of docmd. This version introduces several new features for developers and authors.

chunks.heading: Features
chunks.text: This is the documentation for version 0.5.0 of docmd. This version introduces several new features for developers and authors. - **Developer Tools**: Enhanced debugging capabilities - **Performance**: Faster build times for large documentation sites - **Search**: Improved keyword search with better relevance scoring - **Plugins**: New plugin API for extending functionality

chunks.heading: Getting Started
chunks.text: - **Developer Tools**: Enhanced debugging capabilities - **Performance**: Faster build times for large documentation sites - **Search**: Improved keyword search with better relevance scoring - **Plugins**: New plugin API for extending functionality To get started with version 0.5.0, install the package: ```bash npm install @docmd/core@0.5.0 ```

chunks.heading: Developer Guide
chunks.text: sites - **Search**: Improved keyword search with better relevance scoring - **Plugins**: New plugin API for extending functionality To get started with version 0.5.0, install the package: ```bash npm install @docmd/core@0.5.0 ``` This version is designed with developers in mind. The new developer tools make it easier to debug and customize your documentation site.

chunks.heading: Configuration
chunks.text: version 0.5.0, install the package: ```bash npm install @docmd/core@0.5.0 ``` This version is designed with developers in mind. The new developer tools make it easier to debug and customize your documentation site. Configure your site by creating a `docmd.config.js` file in your project root.

chunks.heading: Building
chunks.text: is designed with developers in mind. The new developer tools make it easier to debug and customize your documentation site. Configure your site by creating a `docmd.config.js` file in your project root. Run the build command to generate your documentation: ```bash npx docmd build ```
